summary: End-to-end testing of Tic Tac Toe app. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests pass (root, /api/matches GET/POST/DELETE, validation, sort order, limit, no _id leak). Frontend: All critical user flows verified — PvP X & O wins, winning-line SVG, draw detection, score tracking & reset, new-round preserves scores, mode switching resets board, CPU Easy & Hard both respond, Hard CPU beat the simulated human (minimax confirmed), match history POST + DELETE flows, player-name customization. Two minor observations only — see below.

issues: When the user has customized the X-player name and then switches to a CPU mode, the O-input is disabled but its value is NOT auto-replaced with 'CPU Easy' / 'CPU Hard' (because the name-default-override only fires when BOTH names are still defaults). The spec phrasing 'default O name becomes CPU Easy' suggests the rename should happen as long as O is still a default — current logic checks both X and O. Minor UX nit; not a blocker.

Score-panel updates appeared once with O=02 / DRAWS=00 after a sequence of (CPU-Hard win) + (PvP draw) in a single browser session — DRAW counter remained 0 while O count jumped by 2. Likely caused by the CPU AI useEffect firing once more during a fast mode-switch (the cleanup runs but a queued setBoard from playMove can still mutate state). Could not reliably reproduce in a deterministic test; flagging for E1 to review the AI-effect cleanup vs. mode-change race.

action_items: (Optional UX) Consider updating the default-name override logic so that switching to a CPU mode renames the O player to 'CPU Easy/Hard' whenever the O name is still a default, regardless of whether X has been customized.

(Investigate) Verify CPU AI useEffect cleanup truly cancels pending playMove when mode is switched mid-game — there is a potential edge case where a scheduled CPU move can fire on the new (post-reset) board if timing aligns.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py correctly excludes Mongo `_id` via projection {'_id': 0} in both GET endpoints — good.
All endpoints validate with Pydantic Literal types for mode/winner — invalid values return 422 as verified.
TicTacToe.jsx is ~400 lines; still readable but approaching the suggested split threshold. Consider extracting the AI-move useEffect and the persist-match useEffect into custom hooks.
Frontend uses process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL consistently. No hardcoded URLs.
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes — excellent testability.

Minor: useEffect with [board] dependency for winner detection runs on every board update including the reset; current logic is safe because checkWinner returns null on empty board, but could be guarded by `if (!board.some(c => c !== null)) return;` for clarity.
seed_data_creation: Test created TEST_-prefixed match records; auto-cleaned via DELETE /api/matches in pytest fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend API at /api/matches (GET/POST/DELETE) is fully working and validated. Frontend uses data-testid attributes consistently: board-cell-0..8, mode-pvp/easy/hard, player-x-input, player-o-input, score-x, score-o, score-panel, match-history-panel, match-history-list, clear-history-button, new-round-button, reset-all-button, game-status-message, game-board, winning-line, ttt-app, app-title, turn-indicator, mark-x, mark-o. CPU AI takes ~400-600ms — always wait_for_timeout(1500) after a human move in CPU modes. The default-name auto-override only fires when BOTH X and O names are still defaults.
